Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails and Slack for the Heartland overbilling dispute using search_emails with queries related to "Heartland", "dispute", or "HM-2026-Q1-0042" (or similar) and conversations_search_messages with queries related to "Heartland" or "overbilling" (or similar). Expected findings: The original Q1 dispute on invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 identified 3 invalid line items totaling $4,800, Lily Marchetti (cancelled Feb 10), Danielle Osei (cancelled Feb 13), Priya Venkatesh (completed by Swift not Heartland). Marcus put the full $12,800 on payment hold and sent a formal dispute letter to Jake Loomis. 5 of 8 line items are verified legitimate (Dr. Yusuf Abdi, Kevin Tran, Ines Kovac, Marcus Webb, Tanya Reeves).
OE 2: Discover the updated dispute amount from Hana Kim's new findings via Slack message in #finance (C005) and email to Marcus (email_hana_heartland_updated_dispute), both from April 23. Hana found two cross-invoice billing problems: (1) Dr. Yusuf Abdi's Minneapolis-to-Chicago move was billed on two separate invoices, individual bill HM-2026-0418 ($2,150, still outstanding at Balance $2,150 in QuickBooks) and Q1 consolidated bill line 1 ($1,600), the $1,600 is a duplicate charge for the same move; (2) Sandra Kim's Q4 2025 bill HM-2025-Q4-0031 ($1,600) was paid in full (Balance $0 in QuickBooks) but Jake's rate increase letter references an "outstanding balance," flagged as potential double-count. Updated total disputed: $8,000 ($4,800 original + $1,600 Abdi duplicate + $1,600 Sandra Kim carryover). Hana has NOT updated the QuickBooks hold entry, she is waiting for Marcus's sign-off.
OE 3: Search emails for the DOT supplemental documentation request using search_emails with queries related to "DOT", "PHMSA", "Castellano", or "compliance" (or similar). Expected finding: Linda Castellano email (email_dot_supplemental_request, April 24) requesting carrier insurance certificates proving each carrier had valid hazmat liability coverage at time of shipment, plus documentation of shipper's verification of carrier qualifications under 49 CFR 174.7. Deadline remains June 5, 2026.
OE 4: Search Slack for Chloe Vance's message in #operations (C006) about the Heartland carrier insurance gap using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "Heartland insurance" or "hazmat insurance" or "carrier insurance" (or similar). Expected finding (April 25): Swift carries hazmat liability coverage and is sending certificates for Zhao, Tanaka, and Patel moves. Heartland does NOT carry hazmat-specific insurance, their general commercial liability excludes hazardous materials. The Joaquin Reyes solar rig shipment was handled by Heartland. Per Linear, the Reyes shipment is classified as overweight/oversize (not hazmat under 49 CFR), but DOT pulled it into scope because Heartland's transport permit references MoveOps. Chloe is asking Marcus for guidance on disclosure strategy.
OE 5: Search Linear for DOT compliance project using linear_list_issues or linear_list_projects with queries related to "DOT", "compliance", or "hazmat" (or similar). Expected findings: DOT Compliance Audit project with 4 shipments in scope (Mei-Lin Zhao lithium batteries, Keiko Tanaka laser cutter, Raj Patel UPS batteries, Joaquin Reyes solar rig, 3 hazmat-classified + 1 oversize non-hazmat). 3 have complete documentation. 1 (Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic laser cutter) was missing a signed placarding certificate, Blessing Okafor was the coordinator listed in the Linear issue (Airtable lists Javier Morales as assigned coordinator for the relocation record), Swift Relocations was the carrier, the driver verbally confirmed placarding but no signed document was obtained by MoveOps. Swift (Greg Pallone) confirmed they have the signed cert on file and is sending it. $50,000 fine accrual booked in QuickBooks.
OE 6: Search emails for Vectral renewal status using search_emails with queries related to "Vectral", "Rachel Whitfield", "renewal", or "board" (or similar). Expected finding: Rachel Whitfield emailed Mina on April 24 (email_rachel_vectral_board_decline), the board met April 23 and declined the 10% discount. Board views service issues as systemic, not one-time. Would consider renewal at 12% across relocation services with the same terms. Rachel asks for a direct meeting with Elena before formalizing exit. Deadline: May 9, after that Vectral begins formal RFP for Q3 vendor.
OE 7: Search CRM for Vectral deal using crm_search_deals with queries related to "Vectral" (or similar). Expected finding: deal deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk at stage "at_risk" with description updated April 24 noting the board declined 10%, Rachel requesting 12% plus CEO meeting, May 9 deadline.
OE 8: Search emails and Linear for BrightLoop status using search_emails with queries related to "BrightLoop", "Tessa Moreno", or "Simone Richter" (or similar) and linear_list_issues with queries related to "BrightLoop" or "Simone" (or similar), then linear_list_comments with the resulting issueId to retrieve comments on the relevant issue. Expected findings: (1) Tessa Moreno emailed Mina on April 24 (email_tessa_brightloop_ultimatum) threatening to put all future MoveOps relocations on hold if Simone's apartment mismatch is not resolved by end of day April 25, CC'd David Chen, Series B closing, 6 additional relocations at stake. (2) Chloe commented on Linear issue linear_issue_f85be674c9b8 on April 23 (comment_chloe_simone_no_availability), UrbanNest has no 1BR in Back Bay/South End until May 15, options include May 15 unit with ~$2,100 gap housing, expanding search radius, or unit swap negotiation. (3) Airtable record recSimoneRichterBrightloop updated with housing mismatch note and client escalation.
OE 9: Search for vendor capacity constraints using search_emails with queries related to "Lakeline" or "Sandra Walsh" (or similar) and conversations_search_messages with queries related to "Swift" or "capacity" (or similar). Expected findings: Sandra Walsh at Lakeline confirmed coverage of Midwest/Southeast corridors (IL, MI, OH, IN, WI, MN, MO, GA, NC, SC, TN, FL, parts of TX) at approximately 10% below Heartland, but does NOT cover West Coast or international moves, regional backup only, not full national replacement. Swift confirmed capacity for April window and offered 15% volume discount for 10+ moves/quarter, but also imposed an immediate rate increase (15% surcharge plus $500 DOT documentation surcharge per Greg Pallone's pricing update email and Elena's directive in #executive).
OE 10: Search QuickBooks for Heartland financial exposure using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria filtering on Heartland. Expected findings: (1) Q1 consolidated bill HM-2026-Q1-0042 at $12,800 on hold; (2) individual Abdi bill HM-2026-0418 at $2,150 outstanding; (3) Reyes bill HM-2026-0425 at $2,550 ($1,600 base + $950 overweight surcharge) with no hazmat surcharge line; (4) Mosaic damage accrual at $90,000 contingent liability for prototype damage during Heartland-handled Zara Okoye move.
OE 11: Search Airtable for active relocations assigned to Heartland using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records on the Relocations table to identify which in-progress moves depend on Heartland as carrier, assessing near-term dependency if Heartland is dropped.
OE 12: Update the Vectral renewal deal in CRM. Two possible actions: (a) crm_update_deal_stage for deal deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk, change the stage to reflect the worsened status (e.g., from "at_risk" to "closedlost" or similar, since the board rejected the counter-offer and Vectral is moving toward RFP); (b) crm_create_engagement to log the board decline as an engagement note on the Vectral account, recording that the board declined 10% on April 23, Rachel is requesting 12% plus a direct CEO meeting, and the May 9 deadline before formal RFP. Either or both actions satisfy the prompt's request to "update the Vectral deal in CRM if the renewal status has changed."
OE 13: Create Linear tracking tickets (via linear_create_issue) for risks and open items without existing tickets. Candidates include: (1) Heartland carrier insurance gap, no hazmat-specific coverage, relevant to DOT audit and Reyes shipment; (2) BrightLoop service hold threat, Tessa Moreno's April 25 deadline passed with no resolution, 6 future relocations at stake; (3) Heartland dispute scope expansion, total now $8,000, dispute letter needs revision; (4) DOT supplemental documentation, carrier insurance certificates and qualification verification due June 5.
OE 14: Send briefing email (via send_email) to elena.rostova@moveops.com and priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com covering: (1) Heartland dispute updated to $8,000 from $4,800 per Hana's cross-invoice findings; (2) DOT compliance supplemental request requiring carrier insurance certs, Heartland lacks hazmat insurance; (3) vendor capacity, Lakeline covers Midwest/Southeast only (not West Coast), Swift has rate increases; (4) Vectral board declined 10%, wants 12% plus Elena meeting, May 9 deadline; (5) BrightLoop housing unresolved, Tessa threatening hold, no 1BR until May 15; (6) recommendation on Heartland.
OE 15: Post summary in #executive channel (C004) using conversations_add_message covering the key findings, Heartland dispute ($8,000), DOT compliance gaps, vendor capacity constraints, Vectral board outcome, BrightLoop escalation, so Marcus and David are aligned before tomorrow's leadership sync.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
